name: Heart of the Alta Garrotxa - Oix & Beget & Coll d'Ares Tour
name_original: Corazón de la Alta Garrotxa - Vuelta Oix & Beget & Coll d'Ares
region_intro: Hiking around Sant Joan Les Fonts offers exploration of a unique volcanic landscape within the Garrotxa Volcanic Zone Natural Park. The region is characterized by ancient lava flows, impressive basalt columns, and dormant volcanoes. Despite its volcanic origins, the area features lush forests, meandering rivers like the Fluvià and Bianya, and numerous natural springs. This geological diversity provides a range of trails suitable for various fitness levels. Best hiking trails around Sant Joan Les Fonts The most popular hiking route is Route of the 3 volcanic lava flows - Circular in Sant Joan de les Fonts, a 3.5 miles (5.7 km) trail that takes 1 hour 30 minutes to complete. This route showcases the unique geological formations of three superimposed lava flows and the Fontfreda cliff. Another top favourite among local hikers is Castellfollit de la Roca – Pont Trencat loop from Castellfollit de la Roca, an easy 3.5 miles (5.6 km) path. This trail offers views of the basalt cliff of Castellfollit de la Roca and follows the Fluvià river. Local hikers also love the Castell de Juvinyà loop from Sant Joan les Fonts, a 3.8 miles (6.1 km) trail leading through varied terrain including the medieval heritage site of Estada Juvinyà, often completed in about 1 hour 58 minutes. Hiking around Sant Joan Les Fonts is defined by volcanic terrain, lush river valleys, and historical medieval sites. The network of paths offers options for different ability levels, from gentle walks to more challenging treks. The routes in Sant Joan Les Fonts are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 950 reviews. More than 6000 hikers have used komoot to explore Sant Joan Les Fonts's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Sant Joan Les Fonts?
A: Sant Joan Les Fonts offers over 20 hiking trails, catering to various preferences. You'll find a good mix of easy, moderate, and a few more challenging options to explore the unique volcanic landscape.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on hikes in Sant Joan Les Fonts?
A: The terrain in Sant Joan Les Fonts is primarily defined by its volcanic origins within the Garrotxa Volcanic Zone Natural Park. You can expect to hike across ancient lava flows, observe impressive basalt columns, and traverse areas shaped by dormant volcanoes. Despite this, many trails also meander through lush forests and along river valleys like the Fluvià and Bianya.

Q: Are there any family-friendly hiking routes in Sant Joan Les Fonts?
A: Yes, Sant Joan Les Fonts has several family-friendly routes. The 'Les Fonts i Verlets' route is particularly suitable for families, exploring wetlands, fields, small forests, and natural springs. Many of the 15 easy trails in the area are also great for a leisurely family outing.

Q: Can I find trails with waterfalls in Sant Joan Les Fonts?
A: Yes, the area features natural wonders like the Molí Fondo waterfall, which captivates visitors. Some routes will lead you to or near this beautiful natural feature, adding a scenic highlight to your hike.

Q: Where can I see the unique volcanic features of the area?
A: To experience the region's unique volcanic geology, we recommend the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e924937224/route-of-the-3-volcanic-lava-flows-circular-in-sant-joan-de-les-fonts" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Route of the 3 volcanic lava flows - Circular in Sant Joan de les Fonts&lt;/a&gt;. This trail allows you to traverse solidified lava formations and see the Fontfreda cliff, a striking wall of volcanic rock. You can also explore nearby volcanoes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1134337" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santa Margarida Volcano&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4052842" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Croscat Volcano Scoria Slopes&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there circular hiking routes available?
A: Yes, many of the trails in Sant Joan Les Fonts are circular, offering convenient starting and ending points. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e924937224/route-of-the-3-volcanic-lava-flows-circular-in-sant-joan-de-les-fonts" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Route of the 3 volcanic lava flows&lt;/a&gt; is a popular circular option, as is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20076132" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castell de Juvinyà loop from Sant Joan les Fonts&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What historical landmarks can I discover while hiking?
A: Beyond natural beauty, Sant Joan Les Fonts integrates historical sites into its hiking routes. You can discover the medieval bridge, the Romanesque church (part of an ancient monastery), and the Estada Juvinyà fort, one of Catalonia's oldest Romanesque buildings. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20076132" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castell de Juvinyà loop from Sant Joan les Fonts&lt;/a&gt; specifically leads through the medieval heritage site of Estada Juvinyà.

Q: Is there parking available at the trailheads?
A: Yes, parking is generally available at or near the main trailheads in Sant Joan Les Fonts, especially for popular routes within the Garrotxa Volcanic Zone Natural Park. Specific parking information can often be found on local signage or detailed route descriptions.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Sant Joan Les Fonts?
A: The hiking experience in Sant Joan Les Fonts is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 950 reviews. Hikers often praise the unique volcanic landscapes, the well-maintained paths, and the blend of natural beauty with historical sites.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Sant Joan Les Fonts?
A: Sant Joan Les Fonts is enjoyable for hiking throughout much of the year. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery, with lush vegetation in spring and colorful foliage in autumn. Summer can be warm, but shaded forest trails and routes along rivers provide relief. Winter hiking is also possible, often with crisp air and fewer crowds.

Q: Are dogs allowed on the hiking trails in Sant Joan Les Fonts?
A: Generally, dogs are welcome on many hiking trails in Sant Joan Les Fonts and the Garrotxa Volcanic Zone Natural Park, provided they are kept under control, preferably on a leash, and owners clean up after them. It's always a good idea to check specific park regulations or route details for any restrictions.

Q: Are there any less crowded hiking options in the area?
A: While popular routes like the Ruta de les 3 Colades can see more visitors, the extensive network of paths, including those part of the Itinerànnia network, offers many opportunities for quieter hikes. Exploring some of the moderate or slightly longer trails, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/17562566" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Canadell Tower loop from Sant Joan les Fonts&lt;/a&gt;, can lead to a more secluded experience, especially during off-peak hours or seasons.

Alta Garrotxa village included in the Inventory of the Architectural Heritage of Catalonia.
Located at the head of the river Llierca, today it is a tourist town and second homes.
On the Prats de Molló side, a hard but accessible port, with a few kilometers that allow you to rest and good views halfway up the Col de la Seille.

Municipality of the Ripollés region.
The New Bridge over the river Ter stands out, which gives access to the town and was built back in the 12th century.
You cannot leave the town without trying their cookies or buying local gastronomy products.
